package.brief: HH the Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday. The meeting reviewed bilateral relations and explored ways to expand cooperation, with a focus on economic ties. Regional developments in the Middle East were also discussed. The visit underscores the strong diplomatic bonds between Qatar and Pakistan.

article.headline: HH the Amir Receives Prime Minister of Pakistan at Amiri Diwan
article.body:
**Doha, 7 June 2026** — HH the Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday.

During the meeting, the two leaders reviewed bilateral relations between Qatar and Pakistan and discussed ways to enhance cooperation across various fields. The talks focused on expanding economic and trade partnerships, reflecting the shared commitment to deepening ties.

HH the Amir emphasised the value Qatar places on its relationship with Pakistan, stating: "We value Pakistan's stance and look forward to deeper economic cooperation between our two countries."

The discussions also covered regional and international developments, particularly in the Middle East, with both sides exchanging views on issues of mutual concern. The leaders underscored the importance of dialogue and diplomacy in addressing regional challenges.

The visit by Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif comes as part of ongoing high-level exchanges between the two countries, which have maintained strong diplomatic and economic relations for decades. Qatar and Pakistan continue to collaborate in areas such as energy, trade, investment, and labour.

The meeting was attended by senior officials from both sides. The visit reaffirms the commitment of both nations to strengthen their strategic partnership and work together towards shared prosperity and stability in the region.

package.instagram_caption: HH the Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday. The meeting focused on strengthening bilateral relations and expanding economic cooperation. Regional developments were also discussed. #Qatar #Pakistan #Diplomacy #AmiriDiwan #ShehbazSharif #QatarLiving

package.brief: Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art has opened 'Resolutions', an exhibition marking its 15th anniversary. The show examines the museum's foundation through four key areas: its origins under Sheikh Hassan bin Mohammed bin Ali Al Thani, its exhibition history, its engagement with knowledge circulation in the Arab world, and its educational programmes. Entry is free for all visitors. The exhibition underscores Mathaf's role in preserving and promoting modern Arab art.

article.headline: Mathaf Opens 'Resolutions' Exhibition Celebrating 15 Years of Arab Modern Art
article.body:
Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art has inaugurated a new exhibition titled 'Resolutions', marking the institution's 15th anniversary. The exhibition, which opened on 10 June 2026, offers a comprehensive look at Mathaf's foundation and evolution, according to officials.

The exhibition is structured around four defining areas that trace the museum's journey. The first area explores the origins of the collection under the patronage of Sheikh Hassan bin Mohammed bin Ali Al Thani, whose vision laid the groundwork for what would become a leading institution for modern Arab art.

The second area examines Mathaf's exhibition history, showcasing key shows that have shaped its identity. The third area focuses on the museum's engagement with knowledge circulation in the Arab world, highlighting its role in fostering dialogue and scholarship. The fourth area delves into Mathaf's educational programmes, which have engaged diverse audiences over the years.

'Resolutions' reflects Mathaf's commitment to preserving and promoting modern art from the Arab world. The museum, a member of Qatar Museums, has become a vital platform for artists, researchers, and the public.

Entry to the exhibition is free for all visitors, in line with Mathaf's mission to make art accessible to everyone. The exhibition is expected to run for several months, offering ample opportunity for the community to explore the museum's rich history.

As Mathaf celebrates 15 years, 'Resolutions' serves as a testament to the enduring legacy of Arab modern art and the institution's role in nurturing cultural heritage in Qatar and beyond.

package.instagram_caption: Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art opens 'Resolutions', an exhibition celebrating 15 years of preserving and promoting modern Arab art. Explore the museum's origins, exhibition history, knowledge circulation, and educational programmes. Free entry for all! #Mathaf #QatarMuseums #ArabModernArt #Exhibition #Doha #ArtInQatar

package.brief: The Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) has launched a summer mosquito-control programme deploying drones and biological fish-based larvicide across 18 districts in Qatar. The initiative, coordinated with the Ministry of Municipality, aims to reduce mosquito-borne disease risks during warmer months. The programme runs through October and emphasises shared public-health responsibility.

article.headline: Ministry of Public Health Launches Summer Mosquito-Control Programme Across 18 Districts
article.body:
**Doha, Qatar:** The Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) has announced the launch of a comprehensive summer mosquito-control programme, deploying advanced drones and biological fish-based larvicide across 18 districts in Qatar. The initiative, which runs through October, is coordinated with the Ministry of Municipality and aims to curb mosquito populations during the warmer months.

The programme employs drones to survey and treat hard-to-reach breeding sites, while biological larvicide—using fish that feed on mosquito larvae—offers an environmentally friendly control method. According to MoPH officials, these measures target areas with high mosquito activity to reduce the risk of vector-borne diseases.

“Public-health vigilance during summer is a shared responsibility,” said the MoPH Director of Communicable Diseases in a statement. The official urged residents to eliminate standing water around homes and report mosquito breeding sites to authorities.

The Ministry of Municipality supports the programme by coordinating ground-level inspections and community outreach. Together, the two entities aim to ensure comprehensive coverage across residential, commercial, and public spaces.

Mosquito-borne illnesses such as dengue fever and West Nile virus are a global concern, and Qatar’s proactive approach aligns with international best practices. The programme reflects the country’s commitment to safeguarding public health through innovation and collaboration.

Residents are encouraged to take preventive measures, including using repellents and installing window screens. The MoPH will continue to monitor mosquito populations and adjust strategies as needed throughout the summer.

package.instagram_caption: The Ministry of Public Health has launched a summer mosquito-control programme using drones and biological fish-based larvicide across 18 districts. This initiative, coordinated with the Ministry of Municipality, runs through October. Let's do our part to prevent mosquito breeding. #Qatar #PublicHealth #MosquitoControl #SummerSafety #MoPH #QatarLiving
package.story_copy: MoPH launches drone & fish-based mosquito control across 18 districts this summer. #Qatar
package.push_notification: MoPH launches summer mosquito-control programme with drones and fish larvicide across 18 districts.

package.brief: The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 concluded on 24 May, and editorial operations is updating its status from EventScheduled to Completed. The event, which brought together education leaders and stakeholders, has been a key platform for discussing advancements in Qatar's education sector. This update ensures the events page remains accurate and current for users.

article.headline: Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 Concludes, Status Updated to Completed
article.body:
The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 successfully concluded on 24 May, marking the end of a significant gathering for education professionals and stakeholders in Qatar. The event, which was previously listed as scheduled on Qatar Living's events page, has now been moved to a completed status by the editorial operations team.

According to the editorial team, the update ensures that the events page remains accurate and reflects the current lifecycle of the expo. The Qatar Education Leadership Expo serves as a platform for discussions on educational advancements, leadership strategies, and collaboration within the sector.

Throughout the event, participants engaged in sessions and workshops aimed at enhancing educational outcomes in Qatar. The expo aligns with the nation's vision to develop a world-class education system, as outlined in the Qatar National Vision 2030.

With the event now concluded, Qatar Living continues to provide timely updates on key events and activities across the country. The editorial team encourages users to check the events page for upcoming opportunities and resources.

package.instagram_caption: The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 has officially concluded! Stay tuned for more updates on future events. #QatarEducation #EducationLeadership #QatarEvents #EducationExpo #QatarLiving

package.brief: Qatar Airways announced it will resume direct flights to Helsinki, Finland, and Tokyo Haneda, Japan, from July 15, 2026, expanding its global network to over 160 destinations this summer. The move strengthens connectivity between Doha and key markets in Europe and Asia, offering travelers more options and supporting Qatar's aviation hub ambitions.

article.headline: Qatar Airways to Resume Direct Flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda from July 15
article.body:
Qatar Airways has announced the resumption of direct flights to Helsinki, Finland, and Tokyo Haneda, Japan, starting July 15, 2026, further expanding its global network to over 160 destinations this summer.

The Helsinki route will operate four times weekly from July 15, increasing to a daily service from August 1, according to the airline. Tokyo Haneda will receive four weekly flights from July 15, complementing the existing daily service to Tokyo Narita.

"We are pleased to restore connectivity with Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda — two strategically important markets for our network," said Qatar Airways Chief Commercial Officer in a statement.

The resumption of these routes underscores Qatar Airways' commitment to rebuilding its network and providing seamless connectivity for passengers traveling to and from Doha. Helsinki is a key gateway to Scandinavia and the Baltic region, while Tokyo Haneda offers convenient access to central Tokyo, enhancing options for business and leisure travelers.

With the addition of these services, Qatar Airways now operates to over 160 destinations worldwide, reinforcing its position as one of the world's leading long-haul carriers. The airline continues to leverage its Doha hub, Hamad International Airport, to connect travelers across continents.

The move aligns with Qatar's broader strategy to boost tourism and economic ties with Europe and Asia, as the country prepares to welcome more visitors for major events and business opportunities. Tickets for the new flights are available for booking on the Qatar Airways website and through travel agents.

package.instagram_caption: Qatar Airways is expanding its network! ✈️ From July 15, direct flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda resume, offering more travel options to Europe and Asia. With over 160 destinations this summer, your next adventure starts from Doha. Book now! #QatarAirways #Helsinki #TokyoHaneda #TravelQatar #Aviation
